text:
Once upon a time in a small village nestled among gentle hills lived a curious girl named Iris. She loved exploring the woods that bordered theirs an enormous, old oak tree. One afternoon, while climbing up its branches, she discovered a small entrance behind a fallen leaf.

Driven by a burning desire to know what lay beyond, Iris persevered. After what felt like hours of creeping, she pushed aside enough leaves to squeeze her head through. And there she found a library. Not just any library; it was filled with exquisite ancient books.

Each tome felt weightier than an owl. Iris spent days reading them. They whispered tales of stars lost to time, oceans without waves, flowers talking among themselves…and owls. One particular book spoke of an ancient kingdom frozen in time.

In trance-like ecstasy, Iris imagined themselves there—bowsprit aloft on a magnificent gull owl ship. It was medieval knights rescuing fair princesses… except the princess was a wild deer.

After countless nights reading,
